# Welcome to the 256ART Docs

> 256ART is an open platform where artists release generative art that lives fully on-chain, and collectors mint unique outputs created by each artist's algorithm.

- [Welcome to the 256ART Docs](https://docs.256.art/index.md): Welcome to the 256ART Docs
- [Artist Documentation](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/index.md): This section takes you from preparing your artist profile to releasing and managing a fully on-chain generative artwork.
- [Collector Guide](https://docs.256.art/learn-more/index.md): This section contains the operational guides for collecting and viewing work on 256ART. For broader conceptual education, visit 256ART Learn.

## Artist Documentation

- [Why Choose 256ART](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/why-choose-256art.md): 256ART stores and serves the artwork's rendering code, traits, and core ERC-721 metadata through smart contracts. A collector can retrieve that code and metadata from the collection contract without relying on the 256ART website.
- [Getting Started](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/getting-started.md): Before creating a release, you should have:
- [Generative Art Template](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/generative-art-template.md): Use the official template as the starting point for a 256ART release. The two files you prepare for upload are:
- [Artwork Details Form](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/artwork-details-form.md): The Artwork Details Form defines what collectors see and configures the contracts used for your release. Complete the Generative Art Template workflow first, then open an unreleased project in the Artist Portal.
- [Testing Your Project](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/testing-your-project.md): Every project must pass the Artist Portal test before it can be released on Ethereum, Base, or Shape. The test deploys and mints the project in a controlled Sepolia-based environment, allowing you to inspect the contract output without paying production-network gas.
- [Releasing Your Artwork](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/releasing-your-artwork.md): Releasing deploys the production smart contracts and schedules the mint. Deployment creates permanent on-chain records and costs gas, so complete the Artist Portal test and review every setting before signing.
- [OpenSea & Enforcing Royalties](https://docs.256.art/artist-documentation/opensea-enforcing-royalties.md): After a 256ART release is deployed, OpenSea may discover its tokens from the chain automatically. OpenSea's collection page and creator-earnings settings are marketplace configuration; they do not replace the on-chain artwork metadata or the 256ART mint page.

## Collector Guide

- [Understanding and Navigating 256ART](https://docs.256.art/learn-more/understanding-and-navigating-256art.md): 256ART is an open platform for creating and collecting fully on-chain generative art.
- [Collecting Art on 256ART](https://docs.256.art/learn-more/collecting-art-on-256art.md): Minting creates a token from an artist's generative series and assigns it to your wallet. If terms such as wallet, gas, token, seed, or allowlist are unfamiliar, first read Understanding and Navigating 256ART.
- [The Gallery Page](https://docs.256.art/learn-more/the-gallery-page.md): The Gallery Page shows the 256ART tokens held by your connected wallet. From the gallery, you can:
